thinks.think: The text "School Projects" stays centered on the grey chalkboard. A pink flask with blue liquid remains on the right. A grey Viking helmet with brown horns and a yellow band rests on top. A globe on a pink stand emerges in the bottom left corner.

A thick purple border frames the view. The main title shrinks to a small logo in the top left corner. White chalk-style text reading "VO" appears centrally. A rectangular image showing a model volcano is placed on the right. A pink box containing the text "hobbycraft" sits in the bottom left corner.

Starting as "VO", letters are added to form "VOLCA", and finally the complete word "VOLCANO" appears in a rough, handwritten font. The surrounding purple border, corner logos, and rectangular volcano image remain unchanged during this text animation sequence.

summary.text: [0-2] The text "School Projects" appears centered on a grey chalkboard, flanked by a pink flask with blue liquid on the right, a grey Viking helmet with brown horns on top, and a globe on a pink stand in the bottom left. [2-6] A thick purple border frames the view as the title shrinks to a top-left logo. White chalk text animates centrally from "VO" to "VOLCA" and finally "VOLCANO", positioned next to a rectangular image of a model volcano and a pink "hobbycraft" logo in the bottom left. [6-12] The scene transitions to a medium shot of a blonde woman in a light blue t-shirt sitting at a white table with craft supplies, including a rolling pin, clay pack, pens, and a silver board. She speaks to the camera while white shelves behind her display colorful props like a jester hat and a small model house.

[18-20] A model volcano featuring orange and red lava paint flows down its sides onto a textured black square base against a white background, with 'School Projects' and 'hobbycraft' logos in the top corners. [20-26] The view transitions to a static grey chalkboard graphic framed in purple titled 'VOLCANO'. White text lists 'THINGS YOU WILL NEED:' including '01. A SQUARE CAKE BOARD', '02. AIR DRYING CLAY (2 PACKETS)', '03. ORANGE, RED AND BLACK ACRYLIC PAINT', '04. A ROLLING PIN', '07. A JAM JAR', and '08. PAINT PALETTE'. Additional text at the bottom right states: 'FIND THE FULL LIST IN THE DESCRIPTION BELOW OR CHECK OUT THE HOBBYCRAFT BLOG'.

[16-18] A blonde woman in a light blue top with colorful stitching speaks, with a red and green jester hat on shelves behind her. [26-28] A chalkboard graphic titled 'VOLCANO' lists 'THINGS YOU WILL NEED: 01. A SQUARE CAKE BOARD 02. AIR DRYING CLAY (2 PACKETS) 03. ORANGE, RED AND BLACK ACRYLIC PAINT 04. A ROLLING PIN 07. A JAM JAR 08. PAINT PALETTE' and directs viewers to the description or blog. [28-34] The view shifts to hands holding reddish-brown clay over a silver square board on a white table. The hands squeeze and press the clay chunk above the board, preparing to shape it, while a larger block of clay sits to the right.

[34-36] Two hands actively mold a chunk of reddish-brown clay above a silver square board, with a larger block of clay sitting on the white table to the right, while "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos remain in the top corners. [36-40] The camera switches to a top-down perspective as hands use a wooden rolling pin to flatten the reddish-brown clay on the silver board; to the right, a separate clay chunk, several paint bottles, a glass jar, and a white palette rest on the surface. [40-42] The view changes to a side angle revealing a person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt using both hands to lift and shape the flattened clay over the board, with another clay block visible nearby.

[46-54] From a top-down view, hands roll reddish-brown clay on a silver board next to paint bottles, with "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos visible. The camera cuts to a side angle showing a person in a light blue shirt lifting and pressing the flattened clay. Returning to the top-down view, the clay covers most of the silver board as hands smooth the edges. A wooden rolling pin rests vertically beside a smaller clay block, paint bottles, a glass jar, and a white palette. The perspective shifts to a side angle again, revealing the person in a light blue t-shirt leaning forward, gripping the rolling pin to press and smooth the reddish-brown clay sheet across the silver board on the white table.

[44-46] The perspective shifts to a top-down angle where hands use a wooden rolling pin to flatten reddish-brown clay on a silver board, with a block of clay, paint bottles, a glass jar, and a white palette on the white table to the right. [54-56] A large, flat sheet of reddish-brown clay covers the silver board with a wooden rolling pin lying horizontally above, while a person with blonde hair wearing a light blue shirt is visible on the right edge. [56-60] A hand holding a small clear glass jar presses the rim into the flattened reddish-brown clay sheet to cut a circular shape, holding the jar steady while the rolling pin remains visible at the top. 'School Projects' and 'hobby craft' logos are visible.

[60-64] A person in a light blue shirt presses the rim of a small glass jar into a flattened reddish-brown clay sheet on a silver board to cut a shape. A wooden rolling pin is visible at the edge, and logos for "School Projects" and "hobby crafts" are displayed in the corners.
[64-70] The perspective shifts to a top-down view where the glass jar rests centrally on the clay sheet. Hands below roll a separate piece of reddish-brown clay into a long, thin coil on the white table surface. To the right, a wooden rolling pin and a block of clay lie stationary next to the silver board.

[72-76] Hands wrap a long, thin coil of reddish-brown clay around the base of a small glass jar resting on a flat clay sheet atop a silver board, pressing firmly to form a circular wall structure. [76-78] The perspective shifts to a side angle revealing a person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt pressing the clay coil around the jar base centrally located on the larger flat sheet. [78-80] Returning to a top-down view, hands continue smoothing the reddish-brown clay ring around the jar, while a wooden rolling pin and a lump of clay sit on the white table surface to the right. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[80-82] From a side angle, a person with blonde hair wearing a light blue shirt presses a reddish-brown clay coil around the base of a small glass jar resting on a flat clay sheet atop a silver board. [82-86] The camera switches to a top-down view where hands smooth the clay coil around the central clear glass jar. To the right, a wooden rolling pin and a lump of clay sit on the white surface, with "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos in the top corners. [86-88] The view returns to the side angle, showing the person continuing to press the clay coil around the jar base.

[88-96] From a side angle, a person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt presses a reddish-brown clay coil around the base of a small glass jar resting on a flat clay sheet atop a silver board. The camera shifts to a top-down view, showing hands molding the coil to build up the wall structure. The view alternates between these perspectives as the person continues to press and smooth the clay ring around the jar base. The "School Projects" logo appears in the top left, and "hobby craft" is in the top right. The hands work to secure the clay structure around the glass form.

From a side perspective, a person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt presses reddish-brown clay around a clear glass jar. Their hands smooth and shape the clay coil against the jar's surface, building the volcano's structure on a flat clay base atop a white board. They pinch and press the clay upwards against the jar's rim to secure the vertical wall structure. The clay adheres to the jar's sides as they shape it. The "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos remain visible in the top corners.

[70-72] Hands roll a piece of reddish-brown clay into a long, thin coil on a white table surface, then lift and curve it around a glass jar sitting in the center of a flattened clay sheet on a silver board. [104-110] Hands smooth the exterior sides of the reddish-brown clay structure built around the jar, forming a tall, rough cone shape with a central hollow opening. The person, wearing a light blue shirt, uses palms and fingers to press and even out the surface texture of the mound on the flat base. The silver board and white table are visible beneath the clay. Logos for 'School Projects' and 'hobby craft' appear in the top corners.

[110-118] A person wearing a light blue shirt smooths the exterior of a reddish-brown clay volcano structure, pressing the material to refine the cone shape around the central hollow opening. Their hands work to blend the base of the cone into the flat clay sheet, ensuring the mound adheres securely to the white board. The person presses down along the lower edges and connection points to secure the structure, refining the lower section with their fingers. Throughout the process, the "School Projects" logo remains visible in the top left corner and the "hobby craft" logo in the top right corner.

[118-120] Hands smooth the reddish-brown clay volcano structure, pressing the cone base into the flat clay sheet onto the white board to blend the connection. Fingers pat the lower edges for adhesion. [120-122] The hands pick up a thin, rolled strip of reddish-brown clay, lifting the coil from the sheet towards the cone top. [122-126] Placing the strip vertically along the mound's side, they press the top near the opening and smooth downwards, adhering the clay coil to the rough exterior surface to create a ridge on the volcano structure. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[126-130] A person presses a thin, rolled strip of reddish-brown clay vertically down the side of a cone-shaped mound. Their fingers smooth the strip against the rough exterior surface, adhering it from the top rim towards the base to create a raised ridge on the volcano structure. [130-132] The camera shifts to a top-down view of the reddish-brown clay volcano centered on a square base. Hands press and smooth the flat clay surrounding the mound, ensuring the base is even. [132-134] From a top-down perspective, hands hold a thin, rolled strip of reddish-brown clay, positioning it near the top rim of the central crater. The existing vertical strip remains visible on the side of the mound. The "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are displayed in the top corners.

From a top-down perspective, hands position a thin, rolled strip of reddish-brown clay near the top rim of the volcano's central crater, pressing it against the upper edge next to a vertical ridge [134-138]. Subsequently, hands smooth the flat reddish-brown clay base surrounding the central mound, flattening the surface near the bottom left corner and edge [138-142]. A thin, rolled clay strip lies unused on a clear plastic sheet to the right. 'School Projects' and 'hobby craft' logos are visible.

[142-150] From a top-down view, hands work on a square, reddish-brown clay base featuring a central volcano mound with a dark crater. To the right, a rolled clay strip lies on a plastic sheet. The hands press fingers into the flat clay surface, primarily along the bottom edge and moving towards the left. This action creates a textured pattern of small, round indentations across the bottom and left sections of the base, matching the existing texture on the upper left. The central volcano structure remains undisturbed. Logos for "School Projects" and "hobby craft" are visible in the corners.

From a top-down view, hands press fingers into a flat reddish-brown clay base below a central mound, creating a textured surface of small indentations. A rolled clay strip rests on a plastic sheet to the right. The hands systematically texture the base, starting from the lower and left sections, continuing along the bottom edge, and moving to the bottom right. Finally, fingers press into the remaining smooth areas along the bottom edge and corners, extending the pattern across the square sheet while the central volcano mound remains undisturbed. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[158-162] Hands finish pressing finger indentations into the bottom edge of the reddish-brown clay base, lifting away to reveal the fully textured surface surrounding the central volcano mound, then gesture on either side of the square board. [162-166] From a top-down view, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply orange paint around the rim of the central crater on the textured clay volcano model, depositing orange pigment on the clay edge. A white paint palette with orange paint and several small bottles sit on the white table to the right. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[166-174] From a top-down perspective, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply orange paint around the rim of the central crater on a reddish-brown clay volcano model situated on a square base. The view shifts to a close-up side angle as the person continues to brush orange pigment down the textured slopes of the cone, extending the coverage from the rim downwards. A white palette containing orange paint and various bottles rests on the white table to the right, while "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the top corners.

[174-182] From a high-angle shot, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply orange paint to the lower slopes of a reddish-brown clay volcano model. The brush strokes extend the orange coverage down the textured surface towards the flat base board. The hand moves the brush to a white paint palette on the right to reload. A text banner appears reading "Paint The Lava Trails With The Light Orange Colour To Make Them Stand Out". The hand then applies light orange paint to a specific trail on the right side of the volcano model to make it stand out.

[182-184] A hand holding a blue paintbrush applies light orange paint down a lava trail on the right side of the clay volcano, extending the color towards the base while a text banner instructing to paint lava trails remains displayed. [184-192] The perspective shifts to a direct overhead view and the instructional text banner vanishes. The hand uses the blue paintbrush to apply red pigment to the lower left area and textured clay base surrounding the volcano. The red paint spreads across the brown surface, covering the area near the cone's base and extending further outwards towards the edge of the board.

From an overhead perspective, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply red paint along the bottom left edge and corner of a textured clay base. The red pigment spreads onto the brown surface, extending the painted area surrounding a volcano model that features a central crater with yellow and orange tones. Throughout the sequence, the hand continues to brush red paint outward from the model's base. A white palette with orange and red paint sections remains visible on the right side. The 'School Projects' and 'hobby craft' logos are displayed in the top corners.

[200-206] From an overhead perspective, a hand holding a blue paintbrush applies red paint along the bottom edge and corners of the textured clay base. The red pigment spreads onto the brown surface, extending the painted area surrounding the volcano model's base. The white palette with paint sections remains visible on the right. [206-208] The hand then uses the blue brush to apply black paint to the bottom left corner, adding dark pigment near the existing red lava flows. The "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible in the top corners.

[42-44] A person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt uses both hands to press and flatten reddish-brown clay onto a silver square board, forming a base layer. [208-214] From an overhead view, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply black paint to the bottom left corner and along the bottom edge of the textured clay base. The dark pigment spreads adjacent to the central red and orange lava flow design, contrasting with the lighter background. A white palette with red, pink, black, white, and orange paints remains visible on the right. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the corners.

[214-218] From an overhead perspective, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply black paint along the bottom edge of the textured clay base, where the dark pigment spreads horizontally, contrasting with the red lava flow above, while a white palette with various paint colors remains visible on the right. [218-222] From a side angle, a person with blonde hair paints the volcano cone orange using a blue brush on the silver board, adding highlights to the slopes, then extends brush strokes down into the red lava flow area to add orange highlights to the textured surface on the black base. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[224-232] A person with blonde hair is painting a detailed volcano model on a white table. They hold a blue paintbrush and carefully apply streaks of bright orange paint onto the red lava flow area surrounding the base of the volcano. The brush moves deliberately across the textured red surface near the bottom of the orange cone structure, effectively adding highlights to simulate flowing lava. The volcano model rests on a black textured base resembling rocky ground. In the background, a white paint palette with orange and red paint is visible in the upper right corner. Logos "School Projects" and "hobby craft" appear in the top left and right corners respectively. The painting continues as the person refines artistic details of the flow.

[222-240] A person with blonde hair uses a blue paintbrush to apply orange paint streaks onto the red lava flow area of a volcano model. The brush moves across the textured red surface near the base of the orange cone, adding highlights to simulate flowing lava on the black textured base. The white table and a paint palette containing orange, red, and black paints remain visible in the background. The "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are displayed in the top corners. The person continues this detailing process, carefully brushing orange accents onto the red areas to enhance the lava effect.

[240-242] A hand holding a blue paintbrush applies orange paint streaks to the red lava flow area of the volcano model. The brush moves across the textured red surface near the base, adding highlights to simulate flowing lava on the black textured base. [242-250] The view switches to a top-down perspective showing the finished volcano model centered in a rectangular silver foil tray. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the corners. Two hands hold the tray's edges briefly before the right hand reaches toward a clear plastic bottle sitting on the white table surface next to a metal spoon. The right hand lifts the bottle, tilts it over the central crater of the painted volcano model, and pours clear liquid into the black opening surrounded by orange and red paint, while the left hand stabilizes the silver foil tray.

[250-252] From a top-down view, a person pours clear liquid from a plastic bottle into the volcano crater, surrounded by orange and red paint, while stabilizing the silver foil tray. [252-254] The camera cuts to a close-up side view where a person with long blonde hair pours a dark liquid from a small brown bottle into the crater's opening. [254-260] Returning to a top-down perspective, the person uses a metal spoon to deposit white powder from a white container into the central crater. The white substance accumulates inside the black opening, resting atop the liquids within the silver foil tray on the white table surface, near a clear bottle and small brown vial. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the corners.

[260-262] A person adds white powder to the crater using a spoon. The view switches to a close-up showing thick, pinkish foam bubbling up and overflowing down the orange sides of the cone model.
[262-266] The bubbly foam cascades down the orange and red painted slopes, spreading across the black base inside a silver foil tray. It mixes with red liquid and painted lava streaks, covering the top completely.
[266-268] The foam pools into the silver tray, merging with red paint streaks. The view widens to show a blonde woman in a grey sweater behind the volcano. On the table are a vinegar bottle, a cup, and a small bottle. Background shelves hold elf hats. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[270-272] Pinkish foam pools at the base of the model volcano, spreading further into the silver tray. The bubbly mixture merges with red liquid, creating a marbled effect on the surface. The thick flow cascades down orange slopes, covering the black textured ground completely. [272-278] A blonde woman in a light blue top sits at a white table behind the volcano, speaking to the camera with slight mouth movements. The volcano remains covered in pink foam and red streaks within the silver tray. Behind her, white shelves display props like a jester hat. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the top corners throughout.

[278-284] The blonde woman in the light blue top continues speaking to the camera, seated behind a model volcano covered in pink foam and red streaks within a silver tray. Background shelves display a jester hat. [284-286] The scene transitions to a grey chalkboard background framed by a thick purple border. "School Projects" and "hobbycraft" logos appear at the top. Two video thumbnails below display the text "Click Here To Subscribe" and "Click Here For More Projects". The URL "www.hobbycraft.co.uk" is centered at the bottom.

White chalk text animates centrally from "VO" to "VOLCA" and finally "VOLCANO", positioned next to a rectangular image of a model volcano and a pink "hobbycraft" logo in the bottom left. [6-12] The scene transitions to a medium shot of a blonde woman in a light blue t-shirt sitting at a white table with craft supplies, including a rolling pin, clay pack, pens, and a silver board. She speaks to the camera while white shelves behind her display colorful props like a jester hat and a small model house. [18-20] A model volcano featuring orange and red lava paint flows down its sides onto a textured black square base against a white background, with 'School Projects' and 'hobbycraft' logos in the top corners. [20-26] The view transitions to a static grey chalkboard graphic framed in purple titled 'VOLCANO'. White text lists 'THINGS

[200-206] From an overhead perspective, a hand holding a blue paintbrush applies red paint along the bottom edge and corners of the textured clay base. The red pigment spreads onto the brown surface, extending the painted area surrounding the volcano model's base. The white palette with paint sections remains visible on the right. [206-208] The hand then uses the blue brush to apply black paint to the bottom left corner, adding dark pigment near the existing red lava flows. The "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible in the top corners.

[42-44] A person with blonde hair in a light blue shirt uses both hands to press and flatten reddish-brown clay onto a silver square board, forming a base layer. [208-214] From an overhead view, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply black paint to the bottom left corner and along the bottom edge of the textured clay base. The dark pigment spreads adjacent to the central red and orange lava flow design, contrasting with the lighter background. A white palette with red, pink, black, white, and orange paints remains visible on the right. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos appear in the corners.

[214-218] From an overhead perspective, a hand uses a blue paintbrush to apply black paint along the bottom edge of the textured clay base, where the dark pigment spreads horizontally, contrasting with the red lava flow above, while a white palette with various paint colors remains visible on the right. [218-222] From a side angle, a person with blonde hair paints the volcano cone orange using a blue brush on the silver board, adding highlights to the slopes, then extends brush strokes down into the red lava flow area to add orange highlights to the textured surface on the black base. "School Projects" and "hobby craft" logos are visible.

[224-232] A person with blonde hair is painting a detailed volcano model on a white table. They hold a blue paintbrush and carefully apply streaks of bright orange paint onto the red lava flow area surrounding the base of the volcano. The brush moves deliberately across the textured red surface near the bottom of the orange cone structure, effectively adding highlights to simulate flowing lava. The volcano model rests on a black textured base resembling rocky ground. In the background, a white paint palette with orange and red paint is visible in the upper right corner. Logos "School Projects" and "hobby craft" appear in the top left and right corners respectively. The painting continues as the person refines artistic details of the flow.
